payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to select available work
payload.justification: Claiming tasks is an explicit process the enterprise performs to enable child participation in the earning system.

payload.description: Task completion process whereby children fulfill work requirements
payload.justification: Completing tasks is a core process performed by children and reviewed by parents, central to the strategic function.

payload.description: Parental review process to verify child task completion and earnings
payload.justification: SG919 explicitly describes the parental review process as a strategic obligation to verify work and fulfill payment.

payload.description: Payment fulfillment process for discharging parental obligations
payload.justification: Payment obligations represent a strategic process parents perform following review and verification.

payload.description: Task-based earning structure definition function performed by parents
payload.justification: SG916 directly establishes the parental function of defining how tasks translate to earnings—a strategic capability.

payload.description: Visibility provision function showing children available and completed work opportunities
payload.justification: Providing visibility into work opportunities is a strategic function the enterprise performs for child stakeholders.

payload.description: Earnings totalization function displaying weekly accumulated amounts to children
payload.justification: Calculating and displaying weekly totals is a motivational function the enterprise strategically performs.

payload.description: Longitudinal data retention function preserving task and earning history
payload.justification: SG920 establishes data retention as a strategic need, implying a function the enterprise must perform over time.

payload.description: Task representing work available for children to claim and complete
payload.justification: Multiple signals reference tasks as the core work unit that children claim, complete, and parents review—a strategically important thing in the enterprise.

payload.description: Earnings representing monetary value accumulated by children through task completion
payload.justification: Earnings are the financial outcome tracked weekly, reviewed by parents, and retained longitudinally—a core entity of strategic importance.

payload.description: Association between completed tasks and resulting child earnings
payload.justification: The causal relationship between task completion and earnings accumulation is central to the strategic purpose across multiple signals.

payload.description: Weekly earning cycle within which tasks are claimed, completed, and totaled
payload.justification: The week represents a strategic cycle repeatedly mentioned as the timeframe for task availability and earnings accumulation.

payload.description: Child user who claims tasks, completes work, and views earnings
payload.justification: Children are explicitly identified across multiple signals as primary actors performing claiming, viewing, and earning activities.

payload.description: Parent user who defines earning structures, reviews completion, and fulfills payment
payload.justification: Parents are clearly identified as actors with strategic roles in defining, reviewing, and paying within the enterprise context.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through structured task completion
payload.justification: SG915 and SG916 directly express the core strategic goal of facilitating child earnings through defined task structures.

payload.description: Provide children with transparency into available work and completed accomplishments
payload.justification: SG917 articulates visibility as a strategic purpose—a goal to empower children with awareness of opportunities.

payload.description: Motivate children by displaying their weekly earnings progress
payload.justification: SG918 explicitly frames the motivational goal of showing children their accumulated earnings to drive engagement.

payload.description: Support parental accountability in verifying and compensating child work
payload.justification: SG919 establishes the strategic goal of enabling parents to meet their review and payment obligations.

payload.description: Historical data preservation principle for task and earning records
payload.justification: SG920 indicates a strategic need for longitudinal retention, suggesting a principle that data should be preserved over time.

payload.description: Pocket money tracking process through task completion claims
payload.justification: Signal SG922 defines the core business process capability of tracking pocket money via task completion.

payload.description: Parent management function for task entities
payload.justification: Signal SG923 establishes parent management as a business function operating on task entities.

payload.description: Child viewing function for available and completed tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG925 defines viewing capability as a business function enabling children to access task information.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregation function for children
payload.justification: Signal SG926 specifies aggregation as a business function computing weekly earnings totals.

payload.description: Parent reporting function for child-task-earnings data
payload.justification: Signal SG927 defines reporting as a business function enabling parents to view payment-relevant relationships.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly cycle boundaries
payload.justification: Signal SG928 establishes a temporal business rule governing when task availability is reset.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained beyond current period
payload.justification: Signal SG929 specifies a retention business rule requiring preservation of historical task entities.

payload.description: Task entity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG923 explicitly defines task entities as business objects managed by parents with monetary value attributes.

payload.description: Child entity participating in task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG924 establishes child entities as a core business object in relationship with completed tasks.

payload.description: Monetary value attribute of task entity
payload.justification: Signal SG923 specifies monetary value as an explicit attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total attribute for child entity
payload.justification: Signal SG926 defines aggregated weekly earnings as a derived attribute visible to children.

payload.description: Completion relationship between child and task entities
payload.justification: Signal SG924 explicitly establishes a conceptual relationship linking completed tasks to child entities.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings relationship for payment tracking
payload.justification: Signal SG927 defines a three-way relationship between child, task, and earnings entities for payment purposes.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle for task availability and earnings
payload.justification: Signals SG925, SG926, and SG928 consistently reference weekly timeframes as the business cycle governing task and earnings operations.

payload.description: Weekly boundary event triggering task availability reset
payload.justification: Signal SG928 defines the weekly boundary as a temporal business event that triggers task reset.

payload.description: Period boundary event for earnings total calculation
payload.justification: Signal SG926 specifies period boundaries as temporal events at which earnings aggregation occurs.

payload.description: Parent role managing tasks and viewing payment reports
payload.justification: Signals SG923 and SG927 establish parent as a business role with task management and reporting responsibilities.

payload.description: Child role claiming tasks and viewing earnings
payload.justification: Signals SG924, SG925, and SG926 define child as a business role completing tasks and viewing earnings information.

payload.description: Enable tracking of pocket money earned through task completion
payload.justification: Signal SG922 articulates the core business goal of tracking pocket money via task completion claims.

payload.description: Task visibility must be scoped to weekly timeframes
payload.justification: Signal SG925 establishes a business constraint limiting task visibility to weekly periods.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained beyond operational period
payload.justification: Signal SG929 defines a retention constraint requiring preservation of historical data.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Participation Transparency Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to ensure all participants have complete visibility into opportunities, accomplishments, progress toward goals, and status of obligations within the established exchange framework.

payload.name: Opportunity Governance
payload.description: Authority and accountability for defining what work opportunities exist, their associated value structures, and how participants access and claim available commitments within the enterprise framework.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Enterprise obligation to preserve complete longitudinal record of participant actions, commitments fulfilled, and value exchanged for accountability, auditability, and institutional memory.

payload.name: Task Completion Accountability
payload.description: Enterprise commitment to maintaining verifiable records of work performed by participants, ensuring completion is objectively determinable and status is transparently communicated to all accountable parties.

payload.name: Value Exchange Obligation
payload.description: Enterprise responsibility to ensure monetary value is accurately associated with completed work and that fulfillment of payment obligations is transparent, timely, and subject to appropriate oversight.

payload.name: Participant Relationship Governance
payload.description: Enterprise accountability for defining who participates in the work-value exchange framework, what roles govern the relationship, and why this structured exchange model serves the enterprise's foundational purpose.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Responsibility for defining, maintaining, and governing the lifecycle of work items offered within the household, including their monetary value attribution and temporal availability boundaries.

payload.name: Participation Accountability
payload.description: Responsibility for recording and acknowledging individual child participation in household work, including completion claims and eligibility to view opportunities.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: Responsibility for establishing the monetary obligation owed to each child based on completed work, including periodic totaling and payment tracking across weekly cycles.

payload.name: Oversight Reporting
payload.description: Responsibility for providing parental visibility into child participation, task completion, and resulting financial obligations to support household governance decisions.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: Responsibility for preserving longitudinal evidence of task definitions, participation, and earnings beyond operational periods to support accountability and retrospective review.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide participants with visibility into available opportunities and completed accomplishments.
payload.rationale: Integrates the enterprise commitment to transparency (Why-002) with the visibility provision function (How-006) into a single obligation at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall display to participants their accumulated progress toward goals.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the earnings totalization function (How-007) and the motivational goal (Why-003) into an enterprise-level obligation to maintain participant awareness of progress.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a periodic cycle within which opportunities are made available, claimed, completed, and totaled.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise-level temporal governance structure (weekly earning cycle) as a constraint governing the exchange framework without prescribing system implementation.
payload.fit_criteria: Cycle period is defined and all phases (available, claimed, completed, totaled) occur within one cycle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a structure of work opportunities available for participation by household members.
payload.rationale: The Task entity represents the enterprise's commitment to maintain a defined set of work opportunities; at Row 1 this is a structural obligation regarding the composition of the enterprise's opportunity framework.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide a mechanism enabling household members to select and claim available work opportunities.
payload.rationale: The task claiming process represents the enterprise's obligation to enable participant access to work; expressed as an enterprise capability commitment rather than a system function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain authority to define the value structures associated with work opportunities.
payload.rationale: The task-based earning structure definition function and the parent actor's role together establish a governance constraint: the enterprise commits to preserving authority over value-structure definition within a designated role.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish accountability for reviewing work completion and fulfilling associated value commitments.
payload.rationale: The parent actor's responsibility for completion review and payment fulfillment represents an enterprise accountability constraint, ensuring governance over the completion-to-value-delivery cycle.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a complete longitudinal record of participant actions, commitments fulfilled, and value exchanged.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs converge on a single enterprise obligation: the preservation of historical records for task and earning history. CCI-ROW1-C-Why-005 establishes the principle/governance constraint (Why-column), while CCI-ROW1-C-How-008 describes the longitudinal retention function (How-column). At Row 1 abstraction, this is an enterprise commitment to stewardship and accountability, not a system function. The Why-column principle signals Constraint type; the obligation is to 'maintain' (enterprise-appropriate verb) rather than 'retain' (storage/system verb).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of work available for participants to claim and complete.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to maintain the availability of work opportunities as tasks that can be assigned to participants.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a process whereby participants fulfill work requirements to complete claimed tasks.
payload.rationale: Enterprise obligation to provide a defined mechanism through which work is fulfilled and completion is achieved.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable participants to claim available work, complete assigned work, and access their earnings information.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to provide capabilities supporting the full lifecycle of participant engagement with work opportunities and compensation visibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise earnings as monetary value accumulated by children through task completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the enterprise's recognition of earnings as an entity with specific structural characteristics (monetary value associated with children and tasks).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish the association between completed tasks and resulting child earnings.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to create and maintain the relationship between task completion and earnings attribution.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide for parental review to verify child task completion and earnings.
payload.rationale: Governance obligation requiring verification oversight by parents before earnings are confirmed; constrains earnings confirmation to reviewed tasks only.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure fulfillment of payment obligations for verified child earnings.
payload.rationale: Enterprise commitment to discharge payment obligations; derived from the payment fulfillment process which represents the enterprise's accountability for completing value transfer.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a structured framework for work-value exchange between participants.
payload.rationale: Derives from the Why-column goal to enable structured task completion for earning; expresses the enterprise commitment to establish the exchange framework rather than a system function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognize a supervisory participant role accountable for defining work structures, reviewing completion, and fulfilling compensation.
payload.rationale: Who-column actor describing enterprise-scope participant role and its accountability scope; expresses a structural assertion about role recognition and associated responsibilities.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable accountability mechanisms for work verification and compensation fulfillment.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal expressing governance constraint on accountability; the enterprise commits to support (not perform) the accountability rather than describing system behavior.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task offered within the household.
payload.rationale: The task entity and parent management function establish the business responsibility to steward task definitions.

payload.statement: Each task shall have an associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: The monetary value attribute defines a required structural property of the task entity.

payload.statement: The parent shall be responsible for managing tasks and viewing payment reports.
payload.rationale: The parent role establishes business accountability for task stewardship and payment oversight.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task availability within weekly operational cycles.
payload.rationale: The weekly cycle and reset rule establish the business capability to govern task temporal boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall reset task availability at each weekly cycle boundary.
payload.rationale: The weekly boundary event and reset rule define a recurring business responsibility for availability refresh.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task visibility scoped to weekly timeframes.
payload.rationale: The WHY-column constraint establishes a business governance rule on task visibility boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the child as a participant in household work.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child entity as a recognised business role within the participation accountability domain; a structural assertion about business role membership.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's completion of household work.
payload.rationale: Derived from the completion relationship (What-005) and the goal of tracking earnings (Why-001); core business responsibility to record participation.

payload.statement: The child shall be able to claim household work tasks.
payload.rationale: Business capability enabling the child role to initiate participation; derived from the child role's responsibility for claiming tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to the child a view of household work opportunities and completed work.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to provide visibility into available and completed tasks; supports the child's ability to view earnings and select work.

payload.statement: The business shall account for pocket money earned by each child through completion of household work.
payload.rationale: Business accountability for earnings tracking; directly supports the stated goal of enabling pocket money tracking through task completion.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of the total earnings accrued by each child within each weekly period.
payload.rationale: Derived from the weekly earnings total attribute and the weekly operational cycle; the business must be able to maintain periodic earnings records aligned to the defined cycle.

payload.statement: The business shall account for the association between each child, the tasks they have completed, and the corresponding earnings.
payload.rationale: Derived from the child-task-earnings relationship and the pocket money tracking process; the business must maintain accountability for the work-to-payment linkage.

payload.statement: The business shall be able to settle weekly earnings totals for children at the close of each weekly period.
payload.rationale: Derived from the weekly earnings aggregation function and the period boundary event; the business must have the capability to finalize earnings obligations at cycle boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for tracking pocket money earned by children through completion of assigned tasks.
payload.rationale: Derived from the goal to enable tracking of pocket money earned through task completion; expresses the business-level accountability rather than a technical tracking mechanism.

payload.statement: The business shall provide parents with visibility into child participation, task completion, and resulting financial obligations.
payload.rationale: How-column function combined with Who-column role accountability; parent role requires reporting capability as a business responsibility to support household governance.

payload.statement: The parent shall be accountable for managing tasks assigned to children.
payload.rationale: Who-column role defining the parent's structural relationship to task management; establishes business accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task definitions, participation, and earnings beyond the operational period in which they occur.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs establish the business responsibility to preserve historical task data beyond current operational periods. The How-column CCI expresses a capability obligation (retention), while the Why-column CCI expresses the governance constraint driving that capability. The unified business responsibility is to maintain longitudinal records for accountability and retrospective review.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Establishes the parental motivation to define task-based earning structures for children.
Articulates the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed work opportunities.
Defines the motivational goal of enabling children to see weekly earnings totals.
Establishes parental purpose of reviewing child earnings and completed tasks to fulfill payment obligations.
Indicates the strategic need for longitudinal task and earning data retention.
Introduces the conceptual scope of the system being described at a high level.
Defines the core business capability of tracking pocket money through task completion claims by children.
Specifies that parents manage task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Establishes a conceptual relationship between completed task entities and child entities.
Defines visibility requirements for children to view available and completed task entities within weekly timeframes.
Specifies aggregation capability for children to view weekly earnings totals at period boundaries.
Defines reporting capability for parents to view child-task-earnings relationships for payment purposes.
Establishes the temporal business rule for task availability reset at weekly cycle boundaries.
Specifies retention requirement for historical task data entities beyond current operational period.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Practitioner SH001
